_comment: Editorial decoration for the TSDynamics system catalogue. The registry (registry.all_systems()) is the SOURCE OF TRUTH for every fact: name, family, category, dim, params, variables, reference, doi, known_lyapunov, _field_shape. This file ONLY decorates: human display names for subcategories/types, prose blurbs, parameter role notes, plot-dt overrides, projection picks for 4D+ systems, and behavior tags. NEVER put computed quantities (Lyapunov spectra, fractal dimensions, Kaplan-Yorke) here — those are computed at build time. Editorial keys that do not match a registry system are ignored harmlessly; registry systems with no editorial entry render without decoration. Seeded from design/brand/data/systems.json and reconciled against the live registry.

type_blurbs.ode: Continuous-time systems. Write the right-hand side; TSDynamics lowers it to the Rust engine and integrates with adaptive Dormand–Prince, implicit BDF, or fixed-step schemes.
type_blurbs.dde: Feedback with a finite memory. Supply the delays and a history function; TSDynamics integrates by the method of steps with dense interpolation of the past.
type_blurbs.sde: Systems driven by noise. Provide drift and diffusion; TSDynamics integrates with Euler–Maruyama or Milstein and returns ensembles, moments, and the deterministic skeleton.
type_blurbs.map: Discrete-time systems. Iterate orbits, cobweb and bifurcation diagrams, and Lyapunov exponents directly — no integration needed.

subcategory_blurbs.chaotic_attractors: Dissipative flows with a positive Lyapunov exponent that settle onto a strange attractor — bounded, aperiodic, sensitive to initial conditions.
subcategory_blurbs.chem_bio_systems: Reaction kinetics and excitable cells. Mass-action and Hill-type nonlinearities give oscillations, bistability, and spikes.
subcategory_blurbs.climate_geophysics: Low-order truncations of atmosphere, ocean, and flow — capturing regime behavior, tipping, and reversals with a handful of modes.
subcategory_blurbs.coupled_systems: Engineered and coupled chaotic flows — generalized Lorenz/Chen-family attractors and interacting units.
subcategory_blurbs.exotic_systems: Minimal or unusual constructions — hyperchaotic, conservative, and engineered attractors of higher dimension.
subcategory_blurbs.oscillatory_systems: Organized around a limit cycle — self-sustained oscillation, relaxation cycles, tori, and forced resonance.
subcategory_blurbs.physical_systems: Mechanical and electrical models from first principles — pendula, circuits, lasers, and few-body motion.
subcategory_blurbs.population_dynamics: Interacting species and resources — cycles, coexistence, and predator–prey coevolution.
subcategory_blurbs.spatial_fields: Spatially extended PDEs reduced to large ODE systems by method-of-lines — pattern formation and spatiotemporal chaos.
subcategory_blurbs.delayed_systems: The rate depends on the state τ ago — a scalar variable becomes effectively infinite-dimensional, giving high-dimensional chaos from one line.
subcategory_blurbs.stochastic_systems: Deterministic skeletons perturbed by Wiener noise — mean reversion, escape, switching, and noise-induced transitions.
subcategory_blurbs.chaotic_maps: Aperiodic, exponentially sensitive orbits — the simplest route to chaos.
subcategory_blurbs.exotic_maps: Strange and ornamental attractors — iterated trigonometric and polynomial maps prized for their structure.
subcategory_blurbs.geometric_maps: Area-preserving and piecewise-linear maps — mixing, horseshoes, circle and toral dynamics.
subcategory_blurbs.polynomial_maps: Low-degree polynomial maps — the canonical setting for period-doubling and renormalization.
subcategory_blurbs.population_maps: Discrete-generation ecology — single-species recruitment and host–parasitoid models.

Lorenz.blurb: The 1963 convection model — three coupled quadratic ODEs whose butterfly attractor launched modern chaos theory.

Rossler.blurb: A single-folded-band attractor built from the minimal nonlinearity — one quadratic term — yet fully chaotic.

Chen.blurb: A dual to Lorenz with the opposite sign convention — a generalized chaotic flow in the Lorenz family.

Chua.blurb: The double-scroll attractor of Chua's circuit — the canonical electronic chaos generator.

Thomas.blurb: Cyclically symmetric flow whose 27 equilibria and labyrinthine trajectories arise from pure sinusoidal feedback.

Halvorsen.blurb: A cyclically symmetric quadratic flow with a tightly wound multi-scroll attractor.

Aizawa.blurb: An ornamental forced attractor with a pinched-torus geometry — the TSDynamics signature shape.

Dadras.blurb: A multi-wing chaotic flow with adjustable scroll count.

NoseHoover.blurb: A conservative thermostatted oscillator — chaos without dissipation, sampling a canonical ensemble.

RabinovichFabrikant.blurb: A flow from plasma physics with stiff, spiky excursions and a delicate attractor.

RikitakeDynamo.blurb: A two-disk dynamo whose chaotic reversals model the geomagnetic field flipping polarity.

Lorenz84.blurb: Lorenz's low-order atmospheric circulation model — regime behavior in the mid-latitude jet.

Lorenz96.blurb: A ring of advectively coupled sites — the standard testbed for atmospheric data assimilation and spatiotemporal chaos.

Rucklidge.blurb: A model of convection in an imposed magnetic field — a compact chaotic flow.

BurkeShaw.blurb: A symmetric chaotic flow derived from the Lorenz template.

KuramotoSivashinsky.blurb: The canonical 1-D model of spatiotemporal chaos — flame fronts and falling films, chaotic in space and time.

param_roles.L: domain length (sets the number of unstable modes)

GrayScott.blurb: A reaction–diffusion system whose spots, stripes, and self-replicating patterns are a landmark of Turing morphogenesis.

SwiftHohenberg.blurb: The prototype pattern-forming PDE — stripes and hexagons from a single scalar field near a finite-wavelength instability.

VanDerPol.blurb: The archetypal relaxation oscillator — nonlinear damping pumps a stable limit cycle.

ForcedVanDerPol.blurb: Van der Pol under periodic forcing — frequency entrainment and the route to chaos.

Duffing.blurb: A driven double-well oscillator — the textbook forced-chaos system with a fractal basin boundary.

MooreSpiegel.blurb: A thermo-mechanical oscillator from stellar convection — aperiodic pulsation.

ShimizuMorioka.blurb: A symmetric reduction of Lorenz-like dynamics near a homoclinic bifurcation.

Torus.blurb: Quasiperiodic motion on a 2-torus — two incommensurate frequencies, no chaos.

Lissajous2D.blurb: A planar Lissajous figure — the elementary two-frequency oscillation.

Lissajous3D.blurb: A three-frequency Lissajous curve traced on a torus.

DoublePendulum.blurb: Two coupled pendula — the iconic mechanically chaotic system, exquisitely sensitive to initial conditions.

HenonHeiles.blurb: A galactic potential whose bounded motion transitions from regular tori to chaos with energy.

Colpitts.blurb: The Colpitts LC oscillator driven into chaos — a real electronic circuit model.

Laser.blurb: A single-mode laser (Haken) — the Lorenz equations in optical disguise.

JerkCircuit.blurb: A minimal third-order 'jerk' circuit — chaos from one nonlinear element.

SwingingAtwood.blurb: The swinging Atwood machine — a mass on a pulley that swings into chaotic motion.

WindmiReduced.blurb: A reduced model of the magnetosphere–ionosphere coupling (WINDMI) — substorm dynamics.

HindmarshRose.blurb: A three-variable neuron model reproducing spiking and bursting with chaotic interspike patterns.

ForcedFitzHughNagumo.blurb: The forced FitzHugh–Nagumo excitable cell — periodic stimulation of a spiking neuron.

Oregonator.blurb: The Field–Körös–Noyes model of the Belousov–Zhabotinsky reaction — chemical relaxation oscillations.

GlycolyticOscillation.blurb: Sel'kov-type glycolytic oscillations — metabolic limit cycles.

ForcedBrusselator.blurb: The Brusselator under periodic forcing — entrainment and chaos in an autocatalytic reaction.

HastingsPowell.blurb: A three-trophic food chain whose tea-cup attractor is a landmark of ecological chaos.

CellCycle.blurb: A model of the eukaryotic cell-division oscillator.

CircadianRhythm.blurb: A molecular circadian clock — entrainable biochemical oscillation.

MackeyGlass.blurb: A single delayed feedback loop modeling blood-cell production — the canonical high-dimensional delay chaos.

param_roles.tau: feedback delay (raise it to climb the period-doubling cascade into chaos)

IkedaDelay.blurb: A delayed optical-cavity model — sinusoidal nonlinearity with memory, chaotic for modest delay.

SprottDelay.blurb: A minimal scalar delay equation engineered for chaos from one nonlinear term.

ScrollDelay.blurb: A delayed feedback circuit producing multi-scroll attractors in the embedding.

PiecewiseCircuit.blurb: A delayed piecewise-linear circuit — chaos from a saturating nonlinearity with memory.

OrnsteinUhlenbeck.blurb: The canonical mean-reverting process — linear restoring drift plus additive noise, the Gaussian stationary benchmark.

GeometricBrownianMotion.blurb: Multiplicative noise — the log-normal process underlying Black–Scholes and population growth under uncertainty.

DoubleWell.blurb: A particle in a bistable potential driven by noise — noise-induced switching between the two wells.

Logistic.blurb: The logistic map — one quadratic line whose period-doubling cascade is the textbook road to chaos.

Henon.blurb: The Hénon map — a 2-D quadratic map whose folded attractor is the canonical strange attractor of dissipative maps.

Tent.blurb: The piecewise-linear tent map — exact, fully chaotic, conjugate to the binary shift.

Chirikov.blurb: The Chirikov standard map — the paradigm of Hamiltonian chaos, mixing tori and chaotic seas.

param_roles.K: kick strength (controls the transition to global chaos)

Ikeda.blurb: The Ikeda map — a spiral strange attractor from a nonlinear optical ring cavity.

Tinkerbell.blurb: An ornamental 2-D quadratic map whose attractor resembles a fairy in flight.

Gingerbreadman.blurb: A piecewise-linear map tiling the plane with quasiperiodic gingerbread-man cells.

Ulam.blurb: The Ulam map (logistic at r=4) — exactly chaotic with a known invariant density.

Baker.blurb: The baker's map — stretch, cut, and stack; the cleanest model of mixing and the horseshoe.

Circle.blurb: The circle map — mode-locking, Arnold tongues, and the devil's staircase.

Chebyshev.blurb: Iterated Chebyshev polynomials — exactly chaotic maps of the interval.

Gauss.blurb: The Gauss (continued-fraction) map — number-theoretic chaos on the unit interval.

Ricker.blurb: The Ricker recruitment map — over-compensatory density dependence driving fishery population cycles and chaos.

MaynardSmith.blurb: A 2-D ecological recruitment map with delayed density dependence.

DeJong.blurb: A Peter de Jong attractor — a sinusoidal iterated map prized for its lacework structure.

KaplanYorke.blurb: The Kaplan–Yorke map — a skew product whose fractal dimension is known in closed form.

GumowskiMira.blurb: The Gumowski–Mira map — accelerator-physics origin, endlessly varied ornamental attractors.
